name: Angiotensin receptor-neprilysin inhibitor (ARNI)
week1_use: Core HFrEF therapy class. Requires 36-hour ACEi/ARB washout before initiation.

name: ACE inhibitor
week1_use: Core HFrEF therapy class for RAS blockade when ARNI not used.

name: Angiotensin receptor blocker
week1_use: Alternative RAS blockade when ACEi/ARNI not tolerated.

name: Evidence-based beta blocker for HFrEF
note: Only bisoprolol, carvedilol, and metoprolol succinate proven mortality benefit in HFrEF trials.
week1_use: Core HFrEF therapy class with blood pressure and heart-rate safety checks.

name: Mineralocorticoid receptor antagonist (MRA)
week1_use: Core HFrEF therapy class requiring potassium and renal function constraints.

name: SGLT2 inhibitor
note: Dapagliflozin and empagliflozin have HFrEF indication regardless of diabetes status.
week1_use: Core HFrEF therapy class requiring renal eligibility and diabetes-aware checks.

name: Loop diuretic
week1_use: Symptom and congestion management class for later reasoning.

name: Thiazide and thiazide-like diuretic
week1_use: Adjunct diuretic therapy, often used with loop diuretics for diuretic resistance.

name: Hydralazine/isosorbide dinitrate combination
week1_use: Alternative for HFrEF when ACEi/ARB/ARNI not tolerated; recommended for self-identified Black patients.

name: If channel blocker (Ivabradine)
week1_use: HFrEF with elevated resting heart rate despite beta-blocker therapy.

name: Soluble guanylate cyclase (sGC) stimulator
week1_use: HFrEF with recent HF hospitalization or IV diuretic use (high-risk HFrEF).

name: Cardiac myosin activator (Omecamtiv mecarbil)
week1_use: HFrEF with reduced LVEF and elevated NT-proBNP; systolic dysfunction.

name: Oral anticoagulant
week1_use: AF stroke prevention; dose adjustment by renal function and patient factors.

name: Antiarrhythmic drugs
week1_use: Rate or rhythm control in atrial fibrillation; contraindicated in some structural heart diseases.

name: Cardiac glycoside
week1_use: AF rate control; symptom improvement in HFrEF (not mortality benefit).

name: Electrolyte replacement
week1_use: Electrolyte correction; monitor K+ especially with MRA use.
